Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Secure Domain Name System (DNS) Deployment 2026 Guide [pdf] 28 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. MSA: Memory Sparse Attention 55 upvotes, 4 comments
  3. Log File Viewer for the Terminal 239 upvotes, 34 comments
  4. A 6502 disassembler with a TUI: A modern take on Regenerator 70 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Autoresearch on an old research idea 395 upvotes, 86 comments
  6. NanoClaw Adopts OneCLI Agent Vault 59 upvotes, 8 comments
  7. Finding all regex matches has always been O(n²) 244 upvotes, 63 comments
  8. Debunking Zswap and Zram Myths 79 upvotes, 17 comments
  9. Claude Code Cheat Sheet 533 upvotes, 168 comments
  10. IRIX 3dfx Voodoo driver and glide2x IRIX port 96 upvotes, 24 comments
  11. BIO – The Bao I/O Co-Processor 67 upvotes, 17 comments
  12. Box of Secrets: Discreetly modding an apartment intercom to work with Apple Home 216 upvotes, 74 comments
  13. iPhone 17 Pro Demonstrated Running a 400B LLM 672 upvotes, 301 comments
  14. Dune3d: A parametric 3D CAD application 202 upvotes, 83 comments
  15. Ripgrep is faster than grep, ag, git grep, ucg, pt, sift (2016) 193 upvotes, 82 comments
  16. curl > /dev/sda: How I made a Linux distro that runs wget | dd 86 upvotes, 32 comments
  17. Show HN: Cq – Stack Overflow for AI coding agents 182 upvotes, 78 comments
  18. LiteLLM Python package compromised by supply-chain attack 277 upvotes, 144 comments
  19. Opera: Rewind The Web to 1996 (Opera at 30) 133 upvotes, 78 comments
  20. FCC updates covered list to include foreign-made consumer routers 394 upvotes, 262 comments
  21. Microsoft's "Fix" for Windows 11: Flowers After the Beating 588 upvotes, 423 comments
  22. LLM Neuroanatomy II: Modern LLM Hacking and Hints of a Universal Language? 23 upvotes, 10 comments
  23. The Jellies That Evolved a Different Way to Keep Time 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. Nanobrew: The fastest macOS package manager compatible with brew 29 upvotes, 17 comments
  25. Missile Defense Is NP-Complete 130 upvotes, 111 comments
  26. Microservices and the First Law of Distributed Objects (2014) 38 upvotes, 26 comments
  27. No-build, no-NPM, SSR-first JavaScript framework if you hate React, love HTML 88 upvotes, 75 comments
  28. I Quit Editing Photos 46 upvotes, 41 comments
  29. So where are all the AI apps? 75 upvotes, 83 comments
  30. The Resolv hack: How one compromised key printed $23M 104 upvotes, 152 comments

Last generated 11:00pm, 24 March 2026. Source here.
